concept: Counterexample / Case That Defeats a General Claim
description: A particular case that meets the stated scope of a general claim but does not have the property that claim says all such cases have. A genuine counterexample refutes the general claim as stated; an irrelevant exception, a case outside the scope, or a merely surprising example does not. Clarifying scope may repair the claim, but it does not erase what the counterexample showed about the original wording.
sound_symbolism: The opening 'phi' links the word to 'phisuwa' (example). Contrasting breath 'whe' turns the case against a generalization, 'ki' draws the decisive boundary, and 'ra' places the disconfirming case clearly before the discussion. Four syllables preserve the concept's layered relation between case and claim.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha phiwhekira ra sherewa whekatelu.' (This counterexample refutes that claim.) The targeted claim may modify the noun possessively: 'sherewa phiwhekira' (a counterexample to the claim). Distinct from an ordinary 'phisuwa' (example) and from any case that merely differs from what was expected.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A single encountered case can loosen attachment to an overgeneralized view and invite a more conditional understanding.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Counterexamples let participants challenge universality through a case rather than through force, status, or personal dismissal.

tags.cognition: a scoped case that disproves a general claim as stated
tags.dialogue: a concrete way to challenge overgeneralization
tags.wisdom: attention to particulars that resist an overly broad rule
